content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the implementation delta between the last review checkpoint and HEAD, excluding `.ralph-burning/`. I found 1 fix-now regression in the new sync-ownership logic.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: The new legacy-marker ownership gate strands pre-journal crash recovery. `sync_own_dirty_if_beads_healthy()` now hard-fails whenever `.beads/.br-unsynced-mutations` is present (src/adapters/br_process.rs:1091-1096), even though the same file is still documented as a dirty signal that should survive until the next successful flush clears it (src/adapters/br_process.rs:36-41). Because the current bead routes recovered proposed-bead replay through this helper (src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3022-3054), any repo upgraded with an old pending marker can no longer auto-recover its interrupted mutation; retries will now fail until an operator manually runs a repo-wide flush or deletes the marker.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/adapters/br_process.rs
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs
remediation_hint: Preserve a migration path for the legacy marker. For example, allow `sync_own_dirty_if_beads_healthy()` to flush legacy-only state when no per-adapter journal proves foreign ownership, or migrate the legacy marker into an owned journal record before enforcing the new ownership check.
   Rationale: This is a user-visible recovery regression introduced by the new ownership enforcement: upgraded worktrees with an old `.br-unsynced-mutations` file can no longer complete the automatic replay path that the codebase previously relied on for crash-safe retries.
